summary: Inspected the recovered M5 task graph on chore/m5-task-graph at f2598fc72562, kept it planning-only, preserved downstream M5 tasks as DRAFT, and routed the graph for independent QA review. No product code, package code, content-source data, dependency, ADR, roadmap, gate-doc, telemetry, server, multiplayer, mod, release, branding, or manual battle implementation work was performed.

tests_run.command: node -e taskctl validate all M5 task files
tests_run.result: taskctl validate all M5 ok count=10.

tests_run.command: node -e M5 JSON DAG acyclicity/dependency/status check
tests_run.result: M5 JSON DAG check ok files=10 edges=21 internalEdges=20 downstreamDraft=9 humanGates=2.

decisions: Accepted the recovered M5 graph for QA review as a planning artifact only; M5-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW and routed to qa_reviewer.

Kept all downstream M5 tasks DRAFT: slice definition, simulation loop, client UI, multi-year AI run, Web/Windows distributable evidence, performance gate, player research, manual node battle decision, and M5 exit Human Gate.
Preserved M5 formal passage as a Human Gate and preserved manual node battle as a separate Human Gate; neither is silently converted into implementation.
Preserved M4_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS as an accepted process limit and did not describe it as systems_architect acceptance.

No ADR is needed for this planning recovery because the graph does not freeze or change architecture; future tasks must write ADRs before architecture, save-format, protocol, platform, dependency, or irreversible migration changes.
risks: M5 implementation tasks are high risk because they later touch deterministic authority state, protocol/save schemas, client read-model surfaces, Electron packaging, and player-study evidence; those risks remain in downstream DRAFT tasks.

Manual node battle, M5 formal passage, release/commercial decisions, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, branding, paid services, and frozen product/platform/core architecture changes remain explicit stop conditions.
The recovered graph is a task DAG, not proof that the playable slice, AI run, performance gate, player research, or distributable evidence exists.
requested_action: Perform independent read-only QA review of the M5 task graph for dependency ordering, DRAFT downstream status, Human Gate preservation, architecture-boundary discipline, explicit exclusions, valid handoff, and absence of product/code scope expansion. Accept or request changes.
